rulings.Case Title: RACHEL BERKOWITZ ET AL VS. SUTTER HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 20, 2026, Line 1. DEFENDANT THOMAS OSBORNE , M.D., VRAD's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off calendar per notice of withdrawal of motion for summary judgment on behalf of Defendants Thomas Osborne and VRAD filed on February 27, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: WAVERLY LATOURETTE VS. KIMPTON HOTEL & RESTAURANT GROUP, LLC, A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal Of Entire Action Pursuant To Rule 3.770 [No Hearing Required 3.770(B)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 20, 2026, Line 2. Plaintiff Waverly Latourette's Motion For Dismissal Of Entire Action (Class Action) Pursuant To Rule 3.770 is GRANTED. Good cause appearing and consistent with California Rules of Court, rule 3.770, the court orders Plaintiff may dismiss the individual and class claims without prejudice. The record demonstrates that dismissal will not prejudice class members, thus no notice to the class is required. Plaintiff must file a request for dismissal on the Judicial Council form and should include a copy of this order with that submission. Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: RODNEY HAMPTON, JR. VS. LUKE O’BRIEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Freeze The Use Of Plaintiff'S Social Equity Status On Any Cannabis Permit, Imposing Terminating Sanctions Against Defendants For Extrinsic Fraud And Interntionally Spoilations Of Tax Documents; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Cause Of Action; Declaration; Exhibits And Damages And Awards
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 20, 2026, Line 4. 2 - Plaintiff Rodney Hampton, Jr.'s Motion To Freeze The Use Of Plaintiff's Social Equity Status On Any Cannabis Permit, Imposing Terminating Sanctions Against Defendants For Extrinsic Fraud And Intentional Spoilation Of Tax Documents This is Plaintiff's sixth frivolous motion in less than three months. "A superior court must evaluate two interrelated factors when ruling on a request for a preliminary injunction: (1) the likelihood that the plaintiff will prevail on the merits at trial and (2) the interim harm that the plaintiff would be likely to sustain if the injunction were denied as compared to the harm the defendant would be likely to suffer if the preliminary injunction were issued." (Smith v. Adventist Health System/West (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 729, 749.) With respect to the merits, Plaintiff does not meaningfully analyze the elements of any asserted claim or explain how the evidence establishes a reasonable probability of prevailing on any claim. The moving papers are notably silent as to how Plaintiff is likely to succeed on the merits. The Court cannot simply presume merit; it must be demonstrated, which hasn't happened here. Nor has Plaintiff established imminent or irreparable interim harm. On this record, the asserted harm is speculative and unsupported. There are myriad other issues with Plaintiff's motion but the court will end its analysis here. Because Plaintiff has not demonstrated a reasonable probability of success on the merits or a likelihood of imminent interim harm, injunctive relief is not warranted. Accordingly, Plaintiff's motion is denied. Prior to the hearing, Defendants shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: BARCLAYS BANK DELAWARE VS. ROBERT ARTHUR JOHNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 20, 2026, Line 5. Before the court is a Motion to be Relieved as Counsel from Counsel Michelle Elison and Open Door Legal, counsel of record for Defendant Robert A. Johnson. The motion is GRANTED. The court has carefully reviewed counsel's motion and declaration and Defendant Johnson's opposition, including his attachments. It is clear the attorney-client relationship is broken. Counsel's motion is not for an improper purpose and the requested relief is supported by the evidence and will not cause undue prejudice. Good cause appearing, the motion is granted. No later than 8:30 am on 3/20/2025, Moving Party must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org an updated proposed order on MC-053 with accurate information inserted, where appropriate. Note the Order will not be effective until Counsel files a Proof of Service of the executed order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
